pi_items.verified_value: BASE 3MM (cranial base) - contains alveolar bone structures in jaw
pi_items.source: DICOM header: BodyPartExamined='BASE 3MM' from patient sample 'CT 0051 - InVesalius Sample'
DICOM analysis shows cranial CT contains voxels at 150 HU in alveolar bone region; overlaps with dental bone quality D4 (150-350 HU)
DICOM analysis shows this range includes trabecular/spongy bone voxels (D4-D3 range); overlaps with part of D3 (350-850 HU) dental classification

pi_items.metadata_value: Custom range 150-700 HU for alveolar segmentation
pi_items.verified_value: Reasonable for alveolar bone segmentation - includes D4 (lower density) and part of D3 (medium density) bone per dental implant literature
pi_items.source: NCBI research: bone density D1>1250, D2:850-1250, D3:350-850, D4:150-350, D5<150 HU

pi_items.verified_value: 226-3071 is documented as standard bone segmentation threshold in InVesalius
pi_items.source: GitHub wiki and project documentation references threshold 226-3071 for bone

pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py lines 88-94 enforce custom threshold matching with tolerance

pi_items.metadata_value: Multiple DICOM slices in CT Cranium dataset
Cranial CT contains wide HU range
pi_items.verified_value: Min: -1024 HU (air), Max: 3033 HU (dense bone), Mean: -575.80 HU

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real medical imaging data from the publicly available CT Cranium dataset (InVesalius sample 0051), which is a genuine cranial CT scan containing alveolar bone structures. The dataset is not synthetic and was downloaded from the InVesalius GitHub repository. The specified HU threshold range of 150-700 is reasonable for alveolar bone segmentation, falling within dental bone density classifications D4-D3 (150-350 and 350-850 HU respectively). The task correctly requires a CUSTOM threshold range (150-700 HU) that is distinct from standard InVesalius presets (Bone: 226-3071, Spongy: 148-661), as verified by the verifier's threshold matching logic.
